commonIssues:
Long queues and language barriers (Marathi focus) cause delays at Sub-Registrar Offices (SROs).

How to fix:

  • Book an appointment online using the e-Step-in facility on the IGR Maharashtra Portal.
  • Use an official translator or standard bilingual templates if you are not fluent in Marathi.

Incorrect/vague medical certificates or missing annexures lead to rejection.

How to fix:

  • Ensure the doctor explicitly states the testator is of "sound mind and capable of making decisions".
  • Always carry original ID proofs (Aadhaar/PAN) for the testator and two non-beneficiary witnesses.

Confusion regarding the online payment format for registration fees.

How to fix:

  • Make payments via the GRAS Portal. Ensure you select the correct payment head for 'Registration of Will'.

A registered Will can still be contested on grounds of fraud, coercion, or suspicious circumstances (e.g., excluding natural heirs without reason).

How to fix:

  • Provide clear reasoning in the Will if excluding natural heirs.
  • Consider video recording the execution process to prove free will and soundness of mind.

Lost original Wills or difficulties transferring property post-demise.

How to fix:

  • Apply for a certified copy from the SRO where it was registered.
  • For property transfer delays, legal heirs may still need to file for Probate or Letters of Administration if disputes arise.
